name: qa-definitions-food
description: QA food micronutrient definitions with AI. Use when auditing samples/definitions-food.json for foods with 0 or missing nutrients, tracking checked nutrient-food pairs in a hash table.
purpose: Use AI to double-check foods that currently show 0 or missing values for selected nutrients in a food definitions sample file. Only review unchecked nutrient-food pairs, then record whether each value is confirmed zero, updated, or skipped.
onInvoke: Explain the purpose: this tool helps QA food definition data by finding foods where a nutrient is 0 or missing, asking AI to verify only pairs not already checked, and recording confirmed_zero, updated, or skipped in a progress hash table.
Find JSON files under samples/ that look like food definition samples.
If exactly one sample exists, ask the user: "Are you QA'ing samples/definitions-food.json?"
If more than one sample exists, ask the user to choose which sample file to audit.
If the user has not already stated which nutrient scope they want, STOP and run intentClarification before starting the workflow. Do not guess.
After sample file and nutrient scope are confirmed, start the QA workflow for the selected sample and nutrient list.

intentClarification.when: User asks to QA food definitions but has not named a specific nutrient, default set, or micro-panel scope.
intentClarification.prompt: Which nutrients should I QA? Pick one:

options.label: One nutrient
options.followUp: Which nutrient? (e.g. magnesium, vitamin D, fiber)
options.resolveTo: A single nutrient from defaultNutrients, microPanelAllNutrients, or a custom key the user names.

options.label: All micro requirements panel nutrients
options.followUp: This is a large pass (~46 nutrients). Proceed with all microPanelAllNutrients?

options.label: Custom list
options.followUp: Which nutrients? List names or keys (comma-separated).
options.resolveTo: User-provided list matched to known nutrient keys.

implicitIntentHints: run qa on all the food
make sure foods get nutrients listed if they have it
intentClarification.implicitIntentDefault: If the user describes a broad audit without naming nutrients, ask whether they mean default eight or all micro requirements panel nutrients before proceeding.

defaultNutrients.label: Magnesium
defaultNutrients.prompt: Magnesium is very important. For all food without magnesium please double check if they have magnesium

defaultNutrients.label: Potassium
defaultNutrients.prompt: Potassium is very important. For all food without Potassium please double check if they have Potassium

defaultNutrients.label: Calcium
defaultNutrients.prompt: Calcium is very important. For all food without Calcium please double check if they have Calcium

defaultNutrients.label: Zinc
defaultNutrients.prompt: Zinc is very important. For all food without Zinc please double check if they have Zinc

defaultNutrients.label: Vitamin D
defaultNutrients.prompt: Vitamin D is very important. For all food without Vitamin D please double check if they have Vitamin D

defaultNutrients.label: Vitamin A
defaultNutrients.prompt: Vitamin A is very important. For all food without Vitamin A please double check if they have Vitamin A

defaultNutrients.label: Vitamin C
defaultNutrients.prompt: Vitamin C is very important. For all food without Vitamin C please double check if they have Vitamin C

defaultNutrients.label: Fiber
defaultNutrients.prompt: Fiber is very important. For all food without Fiber please double check if they have Fiber

microPanelAllNutrients.promptTemplate: {label} is tracked in the micro requirements panel. For all food without {label} please double check if they have {label}

needsCheckRule.condition: value is null, undefined, missing, or exactly 0

workflow: Load the selected sample JSON and the progress hash table. If the hash table file does not exist, create it with { "checked": {} } only after the user agrees to proceed with QA.
Pick the current nutrient from the selected nutrient list. Rotate through nutrients; do not stay on one nutrient forever if the user asks for a broader pass.
Build pending foods by filtering for 0 or missing micros[nutrientKey], then excluding any checked hash key.
If no pending foods remain for the nutrient, move to the next nutrient.
Take a focused batch of 5 to 15 pending foods.
Ask AI to review that batch using the nutrient prompt and exact food names.
For each AI result, record the hash key as confirmed_zero, updated, or skipped.
If updated, patch only that nutrient value for the matching food object.
notes: Do not start QA until both sample file and nutrient scope are confirmed.
If intent is unclear, use intentClarification.options — do not assume default eight or micro panel all.
Use the exact food name from the selected sample file for hash keys and AI prompts.
Do not ask AI about non-zero nutrient values unless the user explicitly asks for a broader audit.
Legitimate zeros should be recorded as confirmed_zero rather than changed.
Fortified foods, protein drinks, cereals, milk, fatty fish, and supplements often need closer review for vitamins and minerals.
Keep edits limited to the selected nutrient unless the user asks for a wider correction pass.
